task_completion.description: Verify the correct dataset is loaded into Oracle Analytics Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'sample_order_lines2023' visible in the Data Elements pane on the left side of the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify a horizontal bar chart visualization has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a horizontal bar chart on the main canvas (bars extending from left to right).

task_completion.description: Verify the chart visualizes Revenue (or Sales) by City.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'City' on the Y-axis (Category) and 'Revenue' or 'Sales' on the X-axis (Values). These should be visible in the Grammar pane or on the chart axes.

task_completion.description: Verify the chart is colored by Region.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Region' in the Color section of the Grammar pane, or observe that the bars are colored differently based on a category.

task_completion.description: Verify the data is filtered to show only the top 10 cities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a filter applied in the Filter bar at the top showing 'Top 10', and count exactly 10 bars visible in the chart.

task_completion.description: Verify the chart is sorted in descending order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the chart to ensure the longest bar is at the top and the shortest bar is at the bottom.

task_completion.description: Verify the workbook is saved with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the name 'Top10Cities' in the top title bar of the Oracle Analytics Desktop window or in a completed Save dialog.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to build the chart.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard UI interactions like drag-and-drop in the Grammar pane, open menus, and visible cursor selections rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the chart reflects the actual dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for real city names from the dataset on the axis labels, ensuring the chart is not a static image or mocked data.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't just create a dummy file via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are screenshots showing the actual Oracle Analytics Desktop application open with the workbook being edited, not just a terminal window creating a '.dva' file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected workbook name is 'Top10Cities' (saved as Top10Cities.dva). The target chart is a horizontal bar chart with exactly 10 bars, sorted in descending order. IMPORTANT: The provided dataset 'sample_order_lines2023.xlsx' contains 'City' and 'Sales', but is missing 'Revenue' and 'Region'. If the agent used 'Sales' instead of 'Revenue', or omitted/substituted 'Region' (or failed to color it), score those specific steps positively or leniently, as it is a limitation of the provided environment data.
